messages.content: Build a custom pipeable operator `logAndMap<T, R>` in RxJS v7 that taps a tag then maps via a projection.
Build a custom pipeable operator `filterMap<T, R>` in RxJS v7 that filters by predicate then maps, composed from `filter` and `map`.
What's the v7 way to write a reusable `throttleLog` operator that logs every emission and then throttles? Show a minimal example.
Explain briefly how a custom pipeable operator stays type-safe in RxJS v7, then show a typed implementation of `withIndex<T>() : OperatorFunction<T, { value: T; index: number }>`.
Write an RxJS v7 custom operator `retryTagged<T>` that wraps `retry({ count, delay })` with a prefix log. Constraints: strict types, no `any`, bounded retries.
Add a `minLength` guard to this custom operator: `export const trim = (): OperatorFunction<string, string> => pipe(map((s: string) => s.trim()));`
Build a higher-order `Observable<Observable<number>>` pipeline in RxJS v7 that flattens with `concatAll` so inner streams run sequentially.
What's the RxJS v7 way to flatten a stream of streams with `mergeAll` bounded to 2 concurrent inner subscriptions? Show a minimal example.
Explain briefly how `switchAll` behaves on a higher-order observable in RxJS v7, then show a typed pipeline.
Build a v7 RxJS pipeline that emits per-user `Observable<Order>` streams and flattens them with `mergeAll` into a single `Observable<Order>`.
Write an RxJS v7 pipeline that uses `concatAll` to run a queue of `Observable<TaskResult>` in strict order. Constraints: typed `TaskResult`, no `any`.
Refactor this v7 snippet to use `switchAll` instead of `switchMap`: `const out$ = src$.pipe(switchMap((id: string) => fetchUser$(id)));`
Build a 1 Hz `timer` in RxJS v7 that emits a running count using `startWith` and `scan`.
What's the RxJS v7 way to build a 250 ms `interval` that emits a rolling sum using `scan` seeded with zero? Show a minimal example.
Explain briefly how `startWith` combines with `scan` to seed a derived value in RxJS v7, then show a typed clock that emits `{ tick: number }` every 500 ms.
Write an RxJS v7 stopwatch that uses `interval(100)` + `scan` + `startWith` to emit elapsed ms. Constraints: strict types, `Observable<number>` output.
Add a `reset$` trigger to this stopwatch so the scan restarts at zero: `const sw$ = interval(200).pipe(startWith(-1), scan((a) => a + 1, 0));`
Build a countdown in RxJS v7 that starts at 10 and decrements every second using `timer(0, 1000)` + `scan`.
What's the RxJS v7 way to suppress duplicate `User` emissions by `id` using `distinctUntilChanged` with a custom comparer? Show a minimal example.
Build a RxJS v7 pipeline that emits `Point { x, y }` values and filters consecutive duplicates using `distinctUntilChanged` with a structural comparer.
Explain briefly how `distinctUntilChanged` with a custom comparer differs from the default reference check in RxJS v7, then show a typed case-insensitive string comparer.
Write an RxJS v7 stream of `Form` snapshots that only emits when the `errors` array changes by length. Constraints: typed Form, `distinctUntilChanged` comparer, no `any`.
Add `distinctUntilChanged` with a custom id comparer to this pipeline: `const feed$ = src$.pipe(map((raw) => toItem(raw)));`
Fix this RxJS v7 snippet so it suppresses consecutive emissions with the same `kind` (the current code only uses default equality): `const kinds$ = src$.pipe(distinctUntilChanged());`
Build an RxJS v7 batching pipeline using `bufferTime(500)` that groups `click$` events into `Click[]` chunks.
What's the RxJS v7 way to buffer exactly N items using `bufferCount`? Show a minimal example that groups `LogLine` values 10 at a time.
Explain briefly the difference between `buffer`, `bufferTime`, and `bufferCount` in RxJS v7, then show a typed example using `buffer` with a closing notifier.
Write an RxJS v7 pipeline that batches sensor readings with `bufferTime(200, null, 5)` (time-OR-count). Constraints: typed `Reading`, no `any`.
Build an RxJS v7 telemetry pipeline: `tap` each `Metric` for logging, then `bufferCount(3)` to ship batches of 3 to an exporter.
Build a sliding window aggregator with `windowTime(1000)` in RxJS v7 that materialises each window to a `number[]` via `toArray`.
What's the RxJS v7 way to use `window(closing$)` to emit per-session `Observable<Event>` streams keyed by a notifier? Show a minimal example.
Explain briefly how `windowTime` differs from `bufferTime` in RxJS v7, then show a typed example that sums per-window values.
Write an RxJS v7 pipeline that uses `windowCount(4)` to group `Packet` values into sub-observables and materialises each to an array.
Build a RxJS v7 pipeline that uses `windowTime(500)` + `scan` inside each window to emit a running count per window. Constraints: typed number, no `any`.
Add a `tap` side-effect that logs window boundaries to this pipeline: `const w$ = src$.pipe(windowTime(1000));`
Build a recursive paginator in RxJS v7 using `expand` that fetches `Page<T>` results until `page.next` is null.
What's the RxJS v7 way to implement exponential polling using `expand` with increasing delays? Show a minimal example.
Explain briefly how `expand` differs from `mergeMap` in RxJS v7, then show a typed recursive tree traversal.
Write an RxJS v7 countdown using `expand` that recursively emits `n, n-1, ..., 0` with a 100 ms delay. Constraints: strict types, `Observable<number>` output, no `any`.
Build an RxJS v7 fetch-until-empty flow with `expand` that pulls batches of jobs from `fetchBatch$` until the array is empty.
